notes: Confirmed Pickleheads slug is 'hayward' (pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/hayward), stating 4 locations / 16 courts: SouthGate Hayward Pickleball Park, Hayward Senior Center Pickleball, Unity Park, and Weekes Community Center-Park -- all 4 are included below. Also included a 5th venue, Matt Jimenez Community Center, which is NOT on Pickleheads but is well-corroborated by at least 8 independent sources (see its own notes); this is why the venues array here (5 locations / 19 courts) exceeds Pickleheads' own stated total. IMPORTANT SOURCING NOTE: the City of Hayward's own government website (hayward-ca.gov) does not run its own parks & recreation department -- a site:hayward-ca.gov search for 'pickleball' returns nothing, and the city's own Parks & Recreation page confirms parks are managed by partner agencies, primarily the Hayward Area Recreation and Park District (HARD, haywardrec.org), a special district covering the City of Hayward plus adjacent unincorporated Alameda County communities (Ashland, Cherryland, Fairview, part of Castro Valley). haywardrec.org -- not hayward-ca.gov -- was therefore used throughout this file as the primary official/'city recreation department' source called for in this research task. TWO candidate venues were investigated in depth and explicitly EXCLUDED as not currently operating or not confirmed real: (1) HASS / Sunset Gymnasium (Hayward Adult School, 22100 Princeton St) once hosted 4 indoor courts with a $4 Sunday drop-in pickleball session (8:30-11:30am) -- but the only listing for it (Places2Play) is flagged 'SUNSET IS CLOSED AT THIS POINT. PLEASE CHECK BACK,' a note last updated May 6, 2021, and the underlying HARD Facebook announcement for that exact program is dated May 11, 2019. No current (2025/2026) source shows this program active, so it is treated as defunct and left out entirely rather than listed as a live venue. (2) Mt. Eden Park / Mt. Eden Tennis Courts (2451 W Tennyson Rd) -- HARD's own renovation project page states pickleball is only 'being considered' as part of an in-progress park renovation (expected complete fall 2026) that might convert some existing tennis courts; it is not built/operating today. One third-party aggregator (Pickleball Plus) listed a 'members-only' pickleball entry at this same address, which could not be independently corroborated anywhere else and most plausibly reflects this same not-yet-built proposal rather than a real, separate private club -- excluded. Also excluded Swimply's Hayward-area results, which are private residential backyard court-rental listings, not public venues, and out of scope for this directory. WebFetch is blocked outright on pickleheads.com (HTTP 403, consistent with prior research elsewhere in this repo); every Pickleheads-sourced fact below came via WebSearch AI-summaries of pickleheads.com pages, cross-checked against haywardrec.org and independent aggregators (Places2Play, Bounce, Pickleballify, DailyPB, Yelp, California Pickleball Association, etc.) wherever possible. Lat/lon for 4 of 5 venues are precise OpenStreetMap Nominatim geocodes computed directly from each venue's exact street address (independently reproducible, not simply carried over from one aggregator's claimed pin); Unity Park's is an approximate Mission-Blvd-corridor point (approxLocation: true) because the park is too new (grand opening August 12, 2025) to be precisely geocoded at the building level yet. A numeric Google Maps cid was confirmed for 2 of 5 venues (Hayward Senior Center Pickleball, Matt Jimenez Community Center) by converting a hex feature id found in a live google.com/maps/place/...data=...!1s0x0:0x... URL to decimal; left null for the other 3 (SouthGate, Unity Park, Weekes) after multiple targeted searches found no equivalent hex-format URL for them -- per instructions, not guessed from a newer ChIJ-style Place ID (one was found for SouthGate via Waze, but that format isn't convertible to a numeric cid without a Places API call). Every address's zip code was cross-checked against an independent geocode because HARD's own site and various aggregators disagreed with each other on more than one venue (see individual venue notes) -- default was to trust whichever zip both HARD's official page AND the independent OpenStreetMap geocode agreed on.

venues.name: SouthGate Hayward Pickleball Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court (acrylic surface), dedicated pickleball courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.skill: Recreational/beginner open play Tue/Thu 6-9pm; intermediate/advanced open play Mon/Wed 6-9pm
venues.weather: Southern East Bay -- warmer and sunnier than Berkeley/Oakland to the north, with noticeably less fog; summer afternoons typically mid-70s to mid-80s°F, mild wet winters, occasional bay breeze at this shoreline-adjacent park.
venues.notes: Pickleheads, HARD's own facility page, Yelp, and the California Pickleball Association listing all agree on 6 dedicated outdoor pickleball courts, free walk-up play, and 8am-10pm daily hours -- no material conflict on core facts. Address zip is inconsistently reported across sources: Pickleheads/Yelp/CA Pickleball Association and an independent OpenStreetMap geocode of the exact street address all say 94545, while two different fetches of HARD's own facility page returned 94541 and 94544 respectively (likely a transcription artifact rather than the underlying page changing) -- went with 94545, the majority/independently-geocoded value. Lat/lon is a precise OpenStreetMap Nominatim geocode of 26780 Chiplay Ave. Could not find a numeric Google Maps cid despite several targeted searches for a /maps/place/...data=...!1s0x... URL; a Waze link surfaced a newer-format Place ID (ChIJPV7Wcq6Wj4ARx7LpLyGUwQs) that isn't convertible to a numeric cid without a Places API call, so googleMapsUrl is left null rather than guessed. HARD's own 'Southgate Park Renovation' project page is stale (most recent documented activity: presentations from March-April 2022) and gives no indication of current construction affecting the courts.

venues.name: Unity Park
venues.surface: Outdoor asphalt, dedicated pickleball courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.hours: Sunrise to sunset; free play with a 1-hour limit when courts are busy
venues.weather: Southern East Bay -- warmer and sunnier than Berkeley/Oakland to the north, with noticeably less fog; summer afternoons typically mid-70s to mid-80s°F, mild wet winters, occasional bay breeze at this shoreline-adjacent park.
venues.notes: Pickleheads, HARD's own facility page, Yelp, and HARD's own grand-opening coverage (August 12, 2025) all corroborate 6 dedicated outdoor asphalt pickleball courts, free play -- no material conflict on core facts. Because the park is very new (open less than a year as of this research date), independent geocoding (OpenStreetMap Nominatim) could not resolve an exact building-level point for 20478 Mission Blvd -- it only resolves to a general point along Mission Boulevard, so lat/lon here is an approximate midpoint near the Mission Blvd & Mattox Rd intersection (approxLocation: true), not a confirmed exact pin; a human with direct map access should re-pin this once it's indexed. A local news source (Castro Valley Forum) and HARD's own project page describe this park as being 'in Ashland' -- Ashland is an unincorporated Alameda County community immediately adjacent to Hayward, served by the same Hayward Area Recreation and Park District and carrying a Hayward, CA mailing address/zip, which is why it's included here rather than excluded -- but a human reviewer may want to confirm this site's convention for HARD-served unincorporated areas before treating it as squarely 'in Hayward.' No Google Maps hex feature id found via search; googleMapsUrl left null.

venues.name: Weekes Community Center-Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court, dedicated pickleball courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.weather: Southern East Bay -- warmer and sunnier than Berkeley/Oakland to the north, with noticeably less fog; summer afternoons typically mid-70s to mid-80s°F, mild wet winters.
venues.notes: Pickleheads, HARD's own facility page, and Yelp agree on 2 dedicated outdoor hard-court pickleball courts, free/first-come-first-served -- no material conflict on core facts. HARD's separate 'Weekes Community Park Renovation' project page (fetched directly) shows the project is still in the community-input/planning phase as of its most recently dated milestone (a board study session referenced for September 15, 2025), not yet under construction, and explicitly notes the park's 'picnic areas and sports courts were renovated in the past two years' -- so the pickleball courts specifically appear to already be relatively fresh rather than mid-renovation. No source states the courts are currently closed, and Yelp shows reviews current through mid-2026, so treated as open, but flagging the pending broader renovation project for awareness -- a human should recheck closer to any announced construction start. Address (27182 Patrick Ave, 94544) is precisely confirmed by both HARD's page and an independent OpenStreetMap geocode, so no zip conflict here (one lower-quality aggregator showed a likely transposed '21782 Patrick Ave,' not used). Could not find a numeric Google Maps cid; left null.

venues.name: Hayward Senior Center Pickleball
venues.surface: Indoor wood gymnasium floor, taped pickleball lines
venues.netType: Portable nets (taped lines, not permanent pickleball striping)
venues.price: $4.00 drop-in fee (Pickleheads describes it as a 'one-time fee')
venues.weather: Southern East Bay -- warmer and sunnier than Berkeley/Oakland to the north, with noticeably less fog; summer afternoons typically mid-70s to mid-80s°F, mild wet winters (indoor court, weather-independent).
venues.notes: Pickleheads and Places2Play independently corroborate 2 indoor wood courts with taped lines and portable nets at the Hayward Area Senior Center, a $4.00 drop-in fee (Pickleheads calls it a 'one-time fee'; Places2Play states the dollar figure directly and adds the specific schedule -- Thursdays 1:15-4:00pm, beginners welcome, loaner paddles available), plus restroom/water/wheelchair-accessible amenities -- no material conflict on core facts. This is a physically distinct facility from Matt Jimenez Community Center (different address ~5.5 miles away, different court count, different independently-derived Google coordinates) despite both being small HARD-run indoor drop-in pickleball offerings with a nominal fee -- verified as separate venues, not a duplicate. Address zip is reported inconsistently across secondary sources (94541, 94544, 94545, and 94546 all appear on different aggregators, and one Yelp page even files this identical street address under 'Castro Valley' rather than Hayward); went with 94541, which is what both HARD's own facility page AND an independent OpenStreetMap geocode of the exact street address agree on. This facility is branded/operated as a 50+ Senior Center, so the pickleball drop-in likely skews toward that population in practice, though no source explicitly states an age requirement for the pickleball sessions themselves (Places2Play instead emphasizes 'beginners are welcome'). Google Maps cid derived from a hex feature id (0x5de9fa2bcfc113d1) found in a live google.com/maps/place URL, converted hex-to-decimal directly -- standard cid derivation, not fabricated.

venues.name: Matt Jimenez Community Center
venues.netType: Portable nets in a shared gymnasium
venues.price: $3 drop-in fee (one aggregator, Places2Play, states $5 instead; see notes)
venues.hours: Drop-in Tuesdays and Thursdays 10:00am-1:00pm. Organizers note the center is primarily opened 'when it's sure to rain' -- i.e. as a weather-contingent indoor alternative rather than a guaranteed every-week session.
venues.weather: Southern East Bay -- warmer and sunnier than Berkeley/Oakland to the north, with noticeably less fog; summer afternoons typically mid-70s to mid-80s°F, mild wet winters (indoor court, weather-independent -- though notably this venue's own open hours are explicitly weather-triggered, see hours).
venues.notes: Not listed on Pickleheads' own Hayward index (which shows only 4 locations/16 courts), but independently and consistently corroborated by at least 8 other sources (Bounce, Pickleballify, Places2Play, PickleballRush, PlayPickleball, Yelp, PickleballFire, Pkkup), all describing the same 3 indoor wood-floor courts at 28200 Ruus Rd, run by Hayward Area Recreation and Park District, with drop-in play Tuesdays and Thursdays 10am-1pm -- no material conflict on core facts (existence, indoor, 3 courts). Included here as a real, independently-confirmed public venue per this research task's step 4 (venues beyond Pickleheads' own list are explicitly in scope); this is why the total in this file (5 locations / 19 courts) exceeds Pickleheads' own stated total (4 locations / 16 courts) -- pickleheadsUrl is null because no pickleheads.com page for it was ever found. Drop-in fee is reported as $3 by the large majority of sources but $5 by Places2Play alone; used $3 as the primary figure and flagged the conflict here rather than silently picking one. One organizer contact (DannyBoyPickleball@gmail.com, referenced across several listings, also associated with SouthGate's beginner clinics) is described as opening this venue mainly as a rain-day indoor fallback rather than a guaranteed weekly session -- worth confirming current cadence directly before relying on it. Google Maps cid derived from a hex feature id (0xbc1826b448b62a2b) found in a live google.com/maps/place URL, converted hex-to-decimal directly.
